title: Pagination, Filtering, Links, Rate Limits, Versioning, and Resumable Work · Go Client Systems · Runtime Recall
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for pagination, filtering, links, rate limits, versioning, and resumable work, producing resumable rate-aware API traversal. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http resumable rate-aware API traversal after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Traverse offset, page-number, cursor, and link pagination while detecting repeats, gaps, and termination. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Encode repeated parameters, arrays, dates, sorting, and filters according to the API contract without manual concatenation. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Coordinate server guidance, local quotas, concurrency, backoff, and caller priorities without synchronized bursts. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Checkpoint Resume Deduplication
steps.instruction:
review case — Go security handoff client follows cursor c5, repeated link c5, and rate limit 50/minute while records change between pages. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
